description: Top 25 errors Arabic speakers make at Level 0, with instant fixes. Reference this when correcting student work.

errors.rule: Adjectives need 'am/is/are' before them. Arabic drops it; English never does.
English = SVO always. Subject FIRST.
He/She/It = verb + s. Always.
Don't combine am/is/are with main verbs. Just: subject + verb.
Singular countable nouns need 'a/an' (first mention) or 'the' (specific).
Can + base verb directly. Never 'can to'.
Use don't/doesn't for verb negation. 'No' is only for nouns (no money).
After doesn't = base form. The 's' moved to 'does'.
One female = she. One male = he. Group = they.
P = lips closed, no voice, puff of air. B = lips closed, voice vibrates.
Yes/No questions need Do/Does at the start.
Singular = there is. Plural = there are.
Don't use both the noun AND the pronoun. Choose one.

errors.wrong: I like to fersit my friend.
errors.correct: I like to visit my friend.
errors.rule: V = top teeth on lower lip + voice. F = same position, no voice.
WH questions also need do/does: Where + do + subject + verb?
Adjectives NEVER get -s in English. Only nouns get plural.
I/he/she/it = was. You/we/they = were.

errors.correct: I like coffee very much. / I really like coffee.
errors.rule: 'Very' goes before adjectives (very good). Before verbs: use 'really' or 'a lot'.
/θ/ = tongue between teeth, blow air. Not 's' or 't'.

errors.correct: I want water. / I want a glass of water.
errors.rule: Water, money, food, information = no 'a'. Use: some, a glass of, a piece of.
English ALWAYS needs a subject. Even when Arabic implies it.

errors.wrong: Yesterday I go to the market.
errors.correct: Yesterday I went to the market.
errors.rule: Time words like 'yesterday' = past tense verbs.
Positive = some. Negative/question = any.
L = tongue touches roof of mouth behind teeth. R = tongue curls back, doesn't touch.
-ed after voiceless sounds = /t/. Only say /id/ after t or d (wanted, needed).

usage_note: Use this index when reviewing student writing and speaking submissions. Reference by rank number in feedback: 'Error #3 — remember the -s rule for he/she/it.'
